closure_ledger.notes: Launch, relaunch and post-auth loaders were captured as first-class transient screens.
Canonical TASK-019 on-screen keypad path was used; raw phone value remains local-only.
Legal WebView was captured with Back/recovery attempts.
Policy WebView was captured with repeated navigation recovery attempts.
OTP entry and wrong-code path were captured. Wrong-code feedback includes a visual bottom popup absent from XML.
Wrong-OTP attempts produced transient visual feedback and retry/frontier states; screenshot evidence is required because the popup is not fully exposed by XML.
Captcha screen was confirmed and not solved or bypassed by automation.
First-run onboarding slides and final transition to post-auth catalog were captured.
Top catalog, left rail, banner area, Continue Game row and first grid focus were captured.
Deep scroll and repeated bottom/no-change segment were captured; complete game-title enumeration is not claimed.
QR decoded into local-only evidence and not opened.
Search screen with on-screen keyboard and popular/empty state captured.
No-results state captured after safe synthetic query.
Search results and first result opening into game detail were captured.
Blank journal persisted after delays; Back no-op and collapsed/expanded rail behavior were captured.
Expanded rail traversal, collapsed empty-content focus, recovery to expanded rail, and later focus-loss/no-op states were captured.
Steam top-up QR was decoded locally, treated as a boundary and not opened.
Support/feedback QR was decoded locally, treated as a boundary and not opened.
Settings cards, app version, logout boundary and focus-loss recurrence were captured.
Promo screen, empty promo list, Back no-op, tap-route no-op and D-pad recovery failure were captured.
Gamepad setup/wait state captured; Close returns to Settings. Reset and device detection were not executed.
Reset and physical gamepad detection were visible branches but were intentionally not executed because they can change device/control state and require a separate approved fixture.
Logout confirmation and subsequent canonical relogin were captured with raw auth values local-only.
Game detail family, Back no-op and detail recovery were captured. Raw server/tariff values remain local-only.
Instruction QR was decoded through the established local QR path and not opened.
Own-Steam toggle/warning flow was captured without external navigation.
Tariff/server selection reached a device-connection gate, not payment or streaming. Back recovery failed and explicit relaunch recovered.
Virtual gamepad QR decoded locally; raw target not published and not opened.
Buy-gamepad card was probed safely and did not open a new screen in this run. Real purchase/payment remains forbidden.
No payment screen was reached through approved safe probes; upstream device gate or no-op was observed instead. Real payment remains out of scope.
Stream/session launch was not started. Device gate prevented session start under safe inventory rules.
Owner-confirmed TV screensaver interruption was captured and app foreground was recovered.
Screen family and list behavior are covered, but enumerating every title/card is a data inventory task, not this screen-family inventory.
No profile edit/delete/account mutation surface was entered; such actions remain forbidden without separate approval.
Legal WebViews were covered. External QR/WebView/payment traversal was intentionally not followed.

anomaly_scope: highlights_only; additional local-only redacted anomaly records exist under ignored evidence roots

anomalies.test_design_implication: Runtime checkpoints must inspect screenshots, not XML alone.
TV visual focus and accessibility focus must be recorded separately.
Back recovery from detail needs regression coverage.
Promo codes needs Back/route-switch/focus recovery tests.
Device gate recovery should prefer the approved explicit app relaunch helper on this target.
Idle/wake recovery should verify foreground plus actionable focus.

final_state.closure_basis: Every currently safe reachable approved screen family is marked covered, blocked by boundary/tooling/external state, or not-run out of scope.
